title: Coco the Rabbit
story_html:
Mia's family moved to a small island last spring, and everything still felt new.

Her grandmother gave her a new pet to help her settle in: a soft gray rabbit named Coco.

Every June, the town held a contest for children and their animals, and Mia signed up right away.

Coco loved to steal carrot slices from Mia's plate, and that always made Mia laugh.

Mia's neighbor, Ben, had a dog that always won, and Mia used to feel jealous of his shiny ribbons.

At first, training Coco seemed easy. Mia would teach Coco to hop through a small hoop, and Coco learned fast.

But one morning, Coco looked sick — she would not eat and just lay still in her cage. Mia felt awful when she noticed how weak Coco had become.

"We need to see a vet right away," her grandmother said, and they left immediately.

The vet said Coco just needed rest, clean water, and a warm bath each evening.

Mia used gentle soap and warm water, careful not to hold Coco too tight.

For several days, Mia stayed close, feeding Coco slowly. Every morning, she would search the yard for the freshest dandelion leaves for Coco to eat.

Slowly, Coco's energy came back.

Mia began to regret signing up so eagerly — it felt selfish to care about a ribbon when Coco's health mattered so much more.

She decided to change her goal: instead of trying to win, she just wanted Coco to explore the yard and feel happy again.

That night, Mia decided to review her notes from a rabbit care book so she would know what Coco needed.

When the day of the event came, Coco hopped through the hoop happily, healthy again. Mia did not win first place, but she did not feel hurt inside at all.

That evening, she picked up her pencil and began to write about the whole adventure in her diary.

regret.def: To feel sorry about something you did or didn't do.

weak.def: Not having much strength or power.

jealous.def: Feeling unhappy because someone else has something you want.

steal.def: To take something that is not yours.

several.def: More than two but not very many.

selfish.def: Caring only about yourself and not about others.

contest.def: An event where people compete to win something.

pet.def: An animal kept at home for company.

hurt.def: To cause pain, or to feel pain.

island.def: Land with water all around it.

review.def: To look at something again carefully to check it.

move.def: To go live in a new place.

tight.def: Held firmly, close, or with little space.

love.def: To like something or someone very much.

write.def: To put words on paper using a pen or pencil.

change.def: To become different, or to make something different.

explore.def: To travel through a place to learn about it.

goal.def: Something you are trying to achieve.

bath.def: An act of washing your whole body.

soap.def: Something used with water to wash and clean.

see.def: To notice something with your eyes.

questions.qtext: In "Mia's neighbor, Ben, had a dog that always won, and Mia used to feel jealous of his shiny ribbons," what does jealous mean?
options: unhappy because someone else has something you want
too tired to care about anything
questions.explain: B — Mia felt unhappy because Ben's dog kept winning ribbons that she wished her own pet could win.

questions.qtext: In "Mia felt awful when she noticed how weak Coco had become," what does weak mean?
options: not having much strength or energy
questions.explain: B — Coco was sick and had lost her strength, which is why Mia was so worried.

questions.qtext: In "Every June, the town held a contest for children and their animals, and Mia signed up right away," what does contest mean?
options: a quiet holiday with no plans
an event where people compete to win something
a kind of medicine for pets
questions.explain: C — The town event let children and their animals compete, which is what a contest is.

questions.qtext: Why did Mia and her grandmother take Coco to the vet right away?
options: Coco ran away from the house
Coco looked sick and would not eat
Coco had already lost the contest
Coco was scared of Ben's dog
questions.explain: B — Coco stopped eating and lay still in her cage, so they rushed her to the vet.

questions.qtext: By the end of the story, how had Mia's feelings about winning changed?
options: She wanted to win even more than before
She decided pets were too much trouble
She cared more about Coco being healthy and happy than about winning
She became angry at Ben for winning first place
questions.explain: C — After caring for sick Coco, Mia realized her pet's health mattered more than any ribbon.
